programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan risks his life to investigate a spate of mysterious deaths at a high school, all of which seem to revolve around one particularly 'helpful' student. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates a fatal car accident, and is baffled when he discovers the dead racing driver had survived a similar mishap only days earlier. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is befriended by a man who has family troubles and repays a favour

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team tries to solve the murder of a teenage boy before his distraught parents take the law into their own hands and seek revenge on a street gang suspected of being responsible. A pocket watch provides Calleigh and Delko with a vital clue and an arrest is made - but the case has a tragic outcome. Former small-screen Superman Dean Cain guest stars in the crime drama

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy intelligence officer, and the choice of murder weapon leads the agents to believe he was killed by a colleague. However, while former Seal Sam threatens to compromise the integrity of the case, evidence suggests the victim may have been selling secrets to the enemy

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry receives an unexplained $2,500 cheque in the mail, leading him to a woman who wants him to defend her daughter, but is then accused of murder herself.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Trivette, Walker, CD and Alex are trapped in a vacant lodge by a drug trafficker seen dealing. Chuck Norris, Noble Willingham and Clarence Gilyard star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The FBI's domestic terrorist task force, including operational psychologist Nate Getz, steps in to help the team when a former marine is suspected of creating a chemical bomb. Crime drama, starring Peter Cambor and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: On her day off, Calleigh narrowly avoids becoming the victim of a carjacking by opening fire on her assailants. However, an error in her judgement causes the death of a bystander. programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks go undercover as a married couple in a quiet suburb as part of an investigation into a Russian sleeper cell. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Unfortunately, he doesn't make things any easier for himself by getting over-involved with a pretty medical student. Drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Saskia Wickham and Gary Mavers

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A straightforward suicide case takes a sinister turn for Paul when he comes to a bizarre conclusion. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant and Michael Melia

Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.desc: A vicar suffers a nervous breakdown at a resident's funeral and Tom's son has trouble with his nymphomaniac wife. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick interrogates Gruber about The Fallen Madonna with the Big Boobies and Rene repays Monsieur Alphonse using forged notes. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Guy Siner and Richard Gibson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard has had enough of Pearl and decides to leave home for ever. However, he soon finds he misses her, so the trio try to reunite the couple. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde, with Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Millionaire environmentalist and scientist Dan Hagen is shot on his boat while out at sea, and all the evidence points toward a robbery gone wrong, but DI Goodman suspects foul play by a crew member. The team soon realises the group are not as close-knit as they initially seem, and with everyone hiding a secret, the pressure is on to get to the bottom of how and why Dan was killed. The case gives the British detective a sudden desire to become a sailor, while Florence surprises the team with her action hero credentials. Caribbean crime drama, starring Kris Marshall and Joséphine Jobert

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The island is rocked when Saint Marie's governor Caroline Bamber drops dead at her leaving party, with the commissioner standing right next to her. As DI Goodman and the team try to deduce how she could have been poisoned without their boss noticing any foul play, they find themselves entering the murky world of island politics, where blackmail, threats and corruption seem commonplace - fuelled by ongoing tension between the French and British. Meanwhile, Humphrey braves the world of online dating, under the guidance of ladies' man Dwayne - and soon finds his perfect match. Lucy Cohu and Nigel Lindsay guest star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad investigates a long-running feud between two family ice-cream businesses, which resulted in a shooting and several armed raids 10 years ago. Standing enlists the aid of his daughter in cracking the case, and she discovers evidence of drug dealing - but Pullman does not approve of her involvement. With Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman, alongside guest star Jodie Kelly

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team attends a triple murder at a farmhouse, where the investigating detective, Supt Tom Byrne, wastes no time making his presence felt. The pathologists soon make a breakthrough when DNA found at the scene matches that of a mysterious female serial killer - although Nikki has cause to question the direction they are heading in. The case is proving difficult for Leo, however, whose mind is in turmoil following the suicide of an old friend. Guest starring Vincent Regan and Shelley Conn

Drama, starring Robert Gwilym and Ian Kelsey

programme.desc: Part two of two. Smithy is distraught when he's refused permission to attend Louise's funeral and loses control when he finds fellow prisoner Marty Johnson setting fire to a photograph of her. programme.desc: Paul faces a dangerous confrontation during a desperate search for an abducted boy whose health is failing. Detective drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Peter Polycarpou

programme.desc: Tom's attempts to entertain his two obnoxious grandchildren prove a washout in more ways than one - so dauntless Diana decides to teach the two underage delinquents a lesson they'll never forget. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A fashion show takes a chilling turn when young model Zoe Mackay is strangled just moments after leaving the catwalk, and while the crew and other models come under suspicion, they all have watertight alibis. To make matters worse, there are no witnesses or DNA evidence, the only clue being a single stocking clasped in the victim's hand. What does it mean - and how was her killer able to strike without being spotted? Meanwhile, Florence tries to help Humphrey improve his social skills with the opposite sex, and JP recognises one of the models as a school crush on at school. But will she remember him?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Dwayne attends the funeral of local barber Cedrik Verga and shortly after is puzzled to receive a message from beyond the grave - a text from the deceased claiming 'I was murdered'. Given that his phone was in the coffin, is this a sick prank or something altogether more sinister? Humphrey agrees to investigate, and they soon find evidence to rule out his death from natural causes. Meanwhile, Humphrey demonstrates his culinary skills to Florence and the rest of the team

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A man wrongly convicted of burning down a school is released, and seeks Halford's help in tracking down the culprit. Standing meets a woman who claims to be his daughter (played by Dennis Waterman's real-life daughter Hannah) but Lane is not convinced she is telling the truth. Pullman is offered a promotion to head of the Murder Squad. Kevin Whately and Joe Absalom guest star

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Meanwhile, an early-morning bath saves Harry's life, and two puzzling corpses are brought in to the centre - a heavily tattooed man and a decomposed body found at the airport. Guest starring Sanjeev Bhaskar, Elyes Gabel and Madeline Duggan

Faced with a potential riot outside, Yvonne Hemmingway makes a bad call which leads to disaster

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sparks fly between Sharon and Phil. Michelle starts to worry about Pat and Geoff, and Ian has big news for Cindy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy gets a chip on her shoulder and Natalie goes looking for friendship in the wrong place. Sharon and Phil tell lies

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Sicknote risks his own life to protect Clare, and Blue Watch manages to get the blaze under control - but Dan and Joe find themselves held at gunpoint by drug dealer Jason

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew returns to Cardale after six months away and asks for his old job back. The others are furious at being given the runaround, having endured months of working with below-par locums, and consider appointing a rival candidate. On the personal front, Will is over the moon at becoming a father _ but there's a dark cloud on the horizon. Rural medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Saskia Wickham, Gary Mavers and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul gets drawn into a case involving a maverick policeman, and becomes doubly determined to move away. As he sets about packing his bags, son Marty gets more than he bargained for while trying to help a homeless girl. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tim Vincent and Tamzin Malleson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is outraged to learn her husband's cousin has been using the cafe as a Greek nightclub, while Dorien's self-esteem is boosted by a passionate new affair. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana grow bored with their cosy lifestyles, and decide to look for jobs. Meanwhile, Bayview boss Harvey comes under attack for not providing residents with adequate heating. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden as the retirement home delinquents

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene conceals the painting of the Fallen Madonna, while the British airmen make another escape attempt, smuggling themselves into a PoW camp and faking their own deaths. However, the plan is jeopardised by the Gestapo, who suspect they are hiding in the cafe. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard is forced to take drastic measures, threatening wife Pearl that he will leave home to join the French Foreign Legion. Long-running comedy, starring Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey is delighted when his Aunt Mary, the woman who inspired his love of puzzle-solving, arrives for a visit. But when she becomes the only witness to a fellow tourist's death - seemingly thrown from his hotel balcony in the middle of the night- she finds herself slap bang in the middle of a murder investigation. Meanwhile, JP takes relationship advice from Humphrey after confessing his feelings for Rosey - leaving him more confused than ever. Wendy Craig, Keith Allen and Susie Amy guest star in the island whodunit

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean cops enter the high-pressure world of fine dining when a famous chef is found stabbed to death the morning after the opening of his new restaurant. It proves to be a tricky investigation as the body was stored in a freezer, so the time of death cannot be identified - and when it turns out he was quite a hot-head in the kitchen, all his staff come under suspicion. Away from the case, Dwayne meets his match in the shape of a troublesome schoolboy, Florence has one last lesson in love for the inspector and JP makes a life-changing decision. Tara FitzGerald and Martin Compston guest star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra takes drastic measures to prevent Jack exacting revenge on the hit-and-run driver who killed his wife. With the team's future under threat, she tries desperately to keep the truth about the incident from shady new recruit DS Karen Hardwick, while working on a 10-year-old murder case to restore the squad's reputation. Amanda Redman and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki is furious with Leo when he re-examines a child abuse case by her former mentor, leading to conflict between the pathologists. But she soon has more to worry about when a workman at the centre reveals how his mother is being manipulated by a convicted serial killer into searching for his hidden teenage victims

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Feelings are running high following the death of Jordan Tomlin and Okaro drafts in a full team to police a sell-out gig at the Bombastic nightclub. Forged tickets swell the crowd to dangerous levels and the punters turn nasty when Emma Keane is forced to arrest a man for possession of drugs. Faced with a potential riot outside, Yvonne Hemmingway makes a bad call which leads to disaster

programme.title: Mamwlad
programme.desc: The story of Margaret Haig Thomas, one of Wales' most prominent suffragettes, the shy girl from Monmouthshire who became a militant campaigner for the vote for women. During a lifetime of adventure 'Lady Rhondda' survived the sinking of RMS Lusitania, was jailed for planting a chemical bomb in Newport, and went on hunger strike in support of her political views. She also became one of the most successful businesswomen of her time. Ffion Hague talks to Haig Thomas's biographer, Professor Angela V John, about her subject's life and how her work continues to inspire females today

programme.desc: Aled Samuel and Dr Greg Stevenson examine the architecture of Gwydir Castle in the Conwy Valley, a yeoman's smallholding in the Garn Dolbenmaen area, a 16th-century gentry house with an immaculate interior on the Llyn peninsula and one of the Principality's foremost Arts and Crafts houses, which was designed by Herbert North

programme.title: Gêm Gartre
programme.desc: Owain Gwynedd hosts the fun sports quiz, which pits super fans against stars from the sporting world and other famous faces. At the end of the programme, the winner will aim to win up to £1,000 for a charity of their choice

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: Magazine programme, featuring stories from all over Wales

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Heno Aur
programme.desc: Angharad Mair and Sian Thomas celebrate 30 years of Heno, looking back at some of the great stories features on the show in 1992

programme.title: Y Sioe Fwyd
programme.desc: Online personality Ameer Rana joins presenter Ifan Jones Evans at the helm, alongside chef Hywel Griffith on the culinary show in which a menu is created inspired by local ingredients

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Pawb a'i Farn
programme.desc: Betsan Powy presents a new series of the topical programme. Various guests join members of the public to discuss Black Lives Matter and its relevance to Wales

programme.title: Stori Pel-Droed Cymru
programme.desc: Author Dewi Prysor charts the history of Welsh football, looking back at some of the colourful and emotional history of the national side

programme.title: Miwsig Fy Mywyd
programme.desc: Tudur Owen talks to renowned pianist Llyr Williams about his life and career. Llyr's performances include classical masterpieces by Mozart, Beethoven, Chopin and Ravel

programme.title: Low Box
programme.desc: Rhydian Morgan finds out how many melons he can fit into his digger's bucket, and Duck puts a tractor through its paces. Plus, Dic Gerstic Bach casts his eye over an expensive piece of farm machinery

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the sales of a terrace house in Hornchurch, a piece of land in Newquay, and a commercial space in Rochdale

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A veteran racing driver is involved in a horrific crash on the finish line, two friends fish a hand grenade out of a river and a young boy alerts his mum to a car fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A revelation spreads through the residents, upsetting many and injuring relationships. Australian drama following life in an inner-city neighbourhood

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a home in the north Cornwall countryside with a budget of £525,000, and also visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield, but a messy encounter with a gull almost ruins one deal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a three-bedroom semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Featuring a family trapped 50 metres up on a south coast tourist attraction, and a rock climber who disobeys his dad, with terrifying consequences. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri help two teams search for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, while presenter Anita Manning looks at the history of the game of marbles

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Baby Patch is taken away, and erratic Ash wants to earn some money, but needs a car to do it

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in south Wales on a budget of £600,000 and tries his hand at catching salmon using the art of lave net fishing in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create pieces for people they have never met, with the trio trying to work out the buyer's style and tastes by looking around their home, beginning by travelling to Lancashire to a renovated worker's cottage

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel to the Welsh border, while silver cutlery, a croquet set, and a 110-year-old bandaged teddy bear go up for auction in Burnley

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present a mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The double-length 30th anniversary episode of the soap, in which Lucy Beale's murderer is unmasked. Nick Cotton's body is found and the truth comes to light for a few residents. Dot is determined to do the right thing and takes matters into her own hands, despite her nearest and dearest trying to find a way to help. Dean sets his revenge plan into motion over at the Vic, Kim goes into premature labour and secrets are revealed about the Mitchells. The shocking true identity of Lucy's murderer comes to light, 10 months after she was killed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is concerned that charismatic locum Drew Nicholson-Heath (guest star John Barrowman) is out to expose him, so he resorts to his old tricks of playing the hero - but he is almost caught out and gaslights a colleague into thinking she made a mistake. A desperate Sacha wants to give Essie a reason to keep on living, but when she insists on preparing for the inevitable, he does something completely out of character. Kian spends the day babysitting Emma, hoping to win back Jac's heart

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes tries to make friends with Dermot's mother-in-law Hillary ahead of the baby's birth, and suggests they go out to dinner together. However, the evening comes to an abrupt end for the two feuding matriarchs when expectant mum Maria goes into labour, and the Browns are forced to rush her to hospital. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle does not let lockdown get in the way of completing judging duties on a new season of Drag Race and arranging a high school graduation ceremony for Miss Rona. Fashion designer Marc Jacobs offers tips on staying creative, while comedian Celeste Barber explains the importance of a cupboard full of booze

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of giving up sex for a month, visiting a psychologist to understand her own desires and investigating the pressures young people experience in their sex lives owing to online porn. She meets a celibate rapper who intends to wait until his wedding night and gets some tips on finding alternative ways to connect with her partner

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A mother and daughter suddenly find their tumble drier has set alight, and it all goes wrong for a top British surfer as he rides a wave

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall guide two teams scouring south-west London for bargains to take to auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to Strawberry Hill House in Twickenham, one of the finest examples of Gothic revival architecture in the UK

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A blood donor session sees some awkward encounters, while Ash annoys a customer. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. Also, cameras follow Border Force officers intercepting potentially lethal medicines and members of the National Crime Agency as they raid the homes of currency counterfeiters. Presented by Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: Lost Land of the Tiger
programme.desc: Alan Rabinowitz, Gordon Buchanan, George McGavin, Steve Backshall and Justine Evans travel to the far-flung corners of the Himalayas in search of tigers, snow leopards and clouded leopards, on an expedition aimed at helping the survival of these creatures. The team gets to work by tracking elusive tigers and setting up remote-controlled cameras to film the animals in action

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The dung beetle, an insect revered by ancient Egyptians, uses the sun, the moon and even the Milky Way to move its prized ball of dung in the right direction. Pigeons are often considered feeble birdbrains, but they have incredible memories that can recall several complex travel routes with amazing accuracy and they even use man-made roads and hedgerows to find the quickest way home. Here, the veteran broadcaster profiles animals with extraordinary abilities for finding the way

programme.title: Laura (1944)
programme.desc: A woman presumed murdered turns up alive and well, confounding the suspicions of the New York detective working on the case, who thought the killer could have been one of her many socialite friends. Meanwhile, he finds himself irresistibly drawn to her as he tries to establish the victim's real identity. Mystery, with Gene Tierney, Dana Andrews, Clifton Webb and Vincent Price

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian go head to head at a car boot sale in West Sussex. where a set of Chinese silver spoons could spell trouble for Jonty

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of the island in the Indian Ocean, off the southeastern coast of Africa. It has an ecosystem that has stood in isolation for millions of years, and produced an array of wildlife that cannot be found anywhere else on the planet. He begins by observing lemurs in their habitat, male red giraffe-necked weevils fighting each other, the courting rituals of chameleons, and the behaviour of spiders and fossas

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Newby Hall, North Yorkshire. Mark Stacey comes across two unusual tie and stick pins and Claire Rawle enthuses about a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The daily struggles and dangers faced by a family of the animals living on the edge of the Kalahari desert. In the first episode, Shakespeare is bitten by a puff adder

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day four of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. England captain Joe Root missed his side's First Test defeat in Southampton due to the birth of his second child, with Ben Stokes taking over as skipper at the Ageas Bowl. However, the Yorkshireman was back in action for the Second Test and - barring injury - was also expected to lead his team out in this final Test

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Michael Ball looks at Victoria Wood's candid views on sex and relationships and her ability to say what many were thinking when it came to romance. Clips include stand up from the Albert Hall, spoof documentary, Mr Right, and moments from her sitcom, dinnerladies. With contributions from Duncan Preston, Anne Reid, Maxine Peake and Shobna Gulati. Plus, one of Victoria's most famous songs, The Ballad of Barry and Freda

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from Linacre College, Oxford pit their wits against a team from the Open University in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Nish Kumar and Rachel Parris. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Sorry
programme.desc: Comedy short, written by and starring Lolly Adefope. An actress who considers herself on the verge of superstardom heads to the London premiere of her first Hollywood film. However, during the journey, she finds herself at the centre of a scandal on the internet that could destroy her career

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Britannia's Burning: Fire on the Bridge
programme.desc: Archive footage and eyewitness accounts are combined to tell the story of the blaze 50 years ago that ravaged the railway bridge over the Menai Straits connecting Anglesey and the mainland. The film also charts the construction of the original bridge and the deaths involved in its rebuilding as a modern road bridge

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles returns with the programme in which three artists are challenged to create pieces for people they have never me, beginning by travelling to Shropshire to meet a buyer who wants to mark her recent return to her roots following a health scare

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Actress Felicity Montagu, musician Lady Leshurr, travel presenter Amar Latif, and TV presenters Gethin Jones and Jeff Brazier enter the studios to demonstrate their culinary skills. Gethin, Jeff and Lady Leshurr head to Pomana's, while Felicity and Amar cook for paying customers at Mortimer House Kitchen in a busy lunchtime service. Then it's back to the MasterChef kitchen to impress John and Gregg with their own creations. Who will have the culinary flair and skills to impress the judges?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The daily struggles and dangers faced by a family of the animals living on the edge of the Kalahari desert. In the first episode, Shakespeare is bitten by a puff adder

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With lockdown affecting new filming, Monty Don is at Longmeadow to take an indulgent, horticultural romp through the Gardeners' World archives. From delphiniums to clematis, irises, orchids and dahlias expert growers share their passion along with hints and tips. Plus, Carol Klein visits a garden in Worcestershire where she discovers picture perfect peonies, Adam Frost travels to the Cotswolds and finds out what happens when plants are brought together with imagination and daring and Nick Bailey offers a guide to the world of Britain's favourite flower - the rose

programme.title: Lost Land of the Tiger
programme.desc: From the base camp in the Himalayas, the team moves out to track down tigers throughout Bhutan. Steve Backshall is pushed to the limits of his physical endurance when he takes on a white-water river in the eastern jungles, and Gordon Buchanan deploys remote cameras and uses a fingerprinting technique to identify individual cats. Meanwhile, biologist Dr George McGavin meets the predator during a trip to India

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Amar Latif presents an appeal on behalf of Retina UK, a charity that provides support and information for those with inherited sight loss, and aslo funds research into potential treatments

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist explores the extremes of birth in the animal kingdom, explaining why the giant panda gives birth to the smallest babies of any existing mammal. He also looks at the kiwi, asking why the bird produces a single egg that is a quarter of its body mass - one of the largest in the world - and almost too big to lay

programme.title: The Miracle of the Bells (1948)
programme.desc: Ailing aspiring actress Olga Treskovna dies before the premiere of her first feature film, and her smitten agent Bill Dunnigan agrees to take her body back to her hometown for burial, as per her last request. Olga's other final wish is for her movie to reach cinemas, despite opposition from studio executives, who are reluctant to introduce a new star who can no longer appear in other films. However, Olga hoped her starring role will help inspire the people of her down-at-heel hometown to seize control of their futures, and Bill teams up with local priest Father Paul to ensure Olga's legacy is fulfilled. Drama, starring Fred MacMurray, Alida Valli and Frank Sinatra

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: An observation of the wildlife that inhabits the narrow band of mountains and rainforest on the island's east coast, from ring-tailed lemurs living on an inhospitable peak, to forest-dwelling sifakas, small primates on the verge of extinction. The programme also focuses on the golden bamboo lemur, which is capable of consuming daily doses of cyanide, and looks at the fruit-rich treetops where red-ruffed lemurs defend their food and territory. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and pays a visit to Europe's last water-powered spade mill. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups come under threat from members of a rival meerkat group, Tosca gives birth and Shakespeare continues to fight for his life after being bitten by a snake

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from day five of the Third Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. Following the conclusion of this match, England's focus switches to the shorter formats of the game, with three ODIs against Ireland taking place in quick succession before a three-Test series with Pakistan starts in early August. They will then play three T20Is against the same opposition in preparation for the T20 World Cup, which begins in Australia in October

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Schloss Elmau, a hotel located deep in the snowy Bavarian Alps that is promoted as providing a cultural hideaway that allows guests the chance to restore their mind, body and soul. Monica joins in one of the meditation sessions held every day for the restaurant staff, and Giles recites poetry and travels to a mountain hut to serve dinner, while dressed in lederhosen. They join the hotel guests for an evening performance featuring world-renowned musicians playing for free

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Rupert Murdoch's involvement in the 2016 US elections and the Brexit referendum, which saw the media mogul rising in prominence after a series of scandals and setbacks, from the death of his mother and the collapse of his second marriage to the loss of several important political allies. The programme examines the resolution of the question of Murdoch's successor and a final multi-billion dollar deal that surprises everyone

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Hal Cruttenden hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before comedy circuit regulars Justin Moorhouse and Tom Stade take the mic

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis presents analysis of the day's events

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh catches up with old friends from Colombia, Haiti and Ethiopia, and showcases memorable moments and unseen footage from his past travels - including a chance to see his disastrous attempt to drive a tuk-tuk

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades tells the story of how mid 20th-century furniture continues to influence the modern-day home by revealing the history behind three classic pieces repaired in the barn

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter demonstrates the ultimate in no-fuss, easy-to-grow strawberries and Chris Beardshaw shows how to prune figs and grapes for summer

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Last night saw actress Felicity Montagu, singer Lady Leshurr and presenters Amar Latif, Gethin Jones and Jeff Brazier enter the fray. But one of them has already been voted out, leaving the other four to cook on. They begin with the pairs task, for which they are split into teams and have to serve up identical dishes - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at the Wellcome Genome Campus in Cambridgeshire

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups come under threat from members of a rival meerkat group, Tosca gives birth and Shakespeare continues to fight for his life after being bitten by a snake

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's next leg begins in Ho Chi Minh City, where he uncovers the traumatic 20th-century history of Vietnam, a country that went from a French colony to a socialist republic. He also braves the city streets on a motorcycle, samples French-Vietnamese fusion food, and tries his hand at the national sport, Da Cau. Moving on to the Annamite Mountains west of Da Nang, Michael joins modern Vietnamese on holiday in a quirky facsimile of a medieval French village. Then, in the tailoring capital of Hoi An, Michael sees how Cham weavers make their distinctive brocade

programme.title: Lost Land of the Tiger
programme.desc: The expedition shifts to high altitude in the mountains of Bhutan as Gordon Buchanan captures footage of a snow leopard cub at more than 5,000m, while his heat-sensing camera picks up a big cat stalking through the camp in darkness. Steve Backshall treks to Tiger Mountain along the Tibetan border, where a remote tribe helps provide a close encounter with an elusive predator. As the expedition concludes, the team presents its findings to the Bhutanese prime minister

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates whether animals and plants have developed an awareness of mathematics, including bamboos that flower exactly the same around the world. Plus, the story of a German horse named Hans that was declared a mathematical genius, but all was not as it seemed

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz show in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's daughter has pups, bringing the Whiskers family to a total of 37. She must decide whether to keep the newborns or allow them to die so her own litter can survive. Meanwhile, wounded Shakespeare becomes separated from the group while searching for food

programme.title: Botham: The Legend of '81
programme.desc: A look back at Ian Botham's cricketing career, marking 30 years since the Ashes series that saw the former all-rounder at the peak of his powers. Botham started the series against Australia in 1981 as England captain, but resigned following a string of poor performances and media criticism, and what followed at Headingley, Edgbaston and Old Trafford led to the series becoming remembered as Botham's Ashes. This programme charts the player's highs and lows on and off the pitch, as well as focusing on his epic fundraising achievements and subsequent broadcasting career. With contributions from David Gower, Viv Richards, Mick Jagger and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling onto the capital city of Madrid, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th century's most shocking works of art, before making his way to the town of Huesca. Michael's final stop is on the border with France at Canfranc station, where he learns of the role the old terminus played in the evacuation of Jews during the Second World War

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: In 1977, Bella Abzug is appointed to head the revived Women's Commission, and has to organise the first ever National Women's Convention in Houston. Phyllis is determined to mobilise her own supporters to attend. Drama, starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: Exhausted from a long-distance drive from Illinois, Alice and Pamela arrive in Houston for the National Women's Conference to find their hotel overbooked and have to share a room. Finding herself surrounding by such a diverse group of women, Alice begins to understand the plight of others and why they are battling for the ERA. Drama, starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton goes on a caving expedition under the summit of Blaven

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades delves deeper into the stories of three previous fixes to show how immigration has shaped the UK, remembering the restoration of a pump organ from the Caribbean, a barber's pole with Cypriot links and an Iraqi samovar

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: a beachcombing scrap metal artist, a nautical resin worker, AND a fairy-tale sculptor try to create seaside-themed masterpieces for buyers from Whitstable

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Series in which the presenter travels around the country exploring the civilisation and culture of the ancient Egyptians. He begins by touring the spectacular underground tombs in the Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest. He gains access to the 150-room necropolis built for the sons of Rameses II, where he considers the skill of the vaults' designers, and goes in search of information on the labourers who slaved to build the palatial constructions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: The Golden Age of Steam Railways
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the visionaries who rescued some of the narrow gauge railways that once served Britain's key industries, including its mines, quarries, factories and docks. Home movies reveal how volunteers set to work to bring the lines and steam engines back to life after they disappeared in the years following the Second World War. Narrated by Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A look back at the glamorous heyday of the jet set, from the 1950s to the 1970s, examining a time when the notion of the rich and famous flying to exotic locations around the world was a source of fascination and admiration for the general public. Former racing driver Jackie Stewart, psychologist Dr Martin Dyer Smith, columnist Ross Benson and one-time Concorde pilot Christopher Orlebar are seen reflecting on society's attitude to celebrity

programme.title: Games Britannia
programme.desc: Benjamin Woolley traces the political and social impact that board games have had in Britain over the past 200 years, discovering how they were exported to the US and adapted to promote the American Dream. Monopoly celebrated wealth in the wake of the Great Depression, and together with Cluedo and Scrabble, formed a holy trinity of family favourites that endure to this day

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank charts the rise and fall of high-rise flats by following the history of one estate - the 19-storey Lincoln in Bow, east London, designed in 1960

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox concludes his examination of the three colours he considers most important to human history by arguing that white, traditionally a symbol of virtue and purity, was warped into a shade that exposed the darkest human instincts. He explores why the colour fascinated 18th-century potter Josiah Wedgwood and art historian Johann Joachim Winckelmann, and examines how in the 20th century it came to be linked with the Italian dictator Mussolini and fascism

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that emphasises a slow river winding through colourful foliage, with the final embers of sunlight ahead

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Series in which the presenter travels around the country exploring the civilisation and culture of the ancient Egyptians. He begins by touring the spectacular underground tombs in the Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest. He gains access to the 150-room necropolis built for the sons of Rameses II, where he considers the skill of the vaults' designers, and goes in search of information on the labourers who slaved to build the palatial constructions

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The life of British archaeologist Howard Carter, who located the tomb of Tutankhamun in 1922. Despite his discovery, he was never honoured in his own country. Dan traces Carter's career and also unearths his original artwork and fascinating diary

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a relaxing summer mountain scene, capturing the serene backdrop of warm water and luxurious green grass

programme.title: The Golden Age of Steam Railways
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the visionaries who helped to rescue Britain's standard gauge railways continues, as further home-shot footage reveals how these volunteers managed to restore the lines that once served the nation's mines, quarries, factories and docks, but fell into disuse from the 1950s onwards. Narrated by Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy visits St Petersburg to chart the extraordinary reign of Catherine the Great, whose meteoric rise saw her seize the Russian throne in 1762 and become the most powerful woman in the world, expanding her empire through military victories overseas. The programme also examines the impact of Napoleon's invasion of Russia in 1812, which saw an eventual victory under the leadership of Catherine's grandson Alexander over France, which provided the Romanov dynasty with its finest hour

programme.title: Russia on Four Wheels
programme.desc: Justin Rowlatt and Anita Rani conclude their separate road trips across Russia from Sochi. In St Petersburg, Anita encounters migrant workers and gay rights protesters before heading north to meet entrepreneurs harvesting caviar from sturgeon and ending her journey in Murmansk. Justin continues east into the Ural Mountains and Asia, spending time with bear hunters and visiting Russia's biggest arms fair

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The participants of a surfing contest acquire exceptional and inexplicable powers after being engulfed by a huge 3km-high wave. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos and Marie Dompnier

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The surviving surfers discover they are endowed with strange powers, while the cloud is about to appear again. Supernatural French drama, starring David Kammenos

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Comedy drama following a learning disabled nine-year-old girl. It's a typical Saturday for Rosie Yates's family, starting with an attempt to get her to the park for some fresh air, but things don't go to plan. Starring David Tennant, Jessica Hynes and Miley Locke

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: It is bath night for Rosie, which - as usual - ends in bubble-filled carnage as both Simon and Emily battle to get an unwilling and slippery girl out of the tub. In the earlier timeline, Emily struggles to connect with Rosie, whereas Simon has a bonding moment, but tensions build between them over whether, given a magic wand, they would change Rosie

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a relaxing summer mountain scene, capturing the serene backdrop of warm water and luxurious green grass

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The life of British archaeologist Howard Carter, who located the tomb of Tutankhamun in 1922. Despite his discovery, he was never honoured in his own country. Dan traces Carter's career and also unearths his original artwork and fascinating diary

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy visits St Petersburg to chart the extraordinary reign of Catherine the Great, whose meteoric rise saw her seize the Russian throne in 1762 and become the most powerful woman in the world, expanding her empire through military victories overseas. The programme also examines the impact of Napoleon's invasion of Russia in 1812, which saw an eventual victory under the leadership of Catherine's grandson Alexander over France, which provided the Romanov dynasty with its finest hour

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank travels to the desolate ruins of Amarna in search of the truth about Akhenaten, the most radical pharaoh ever to rule Egypt. He converted the nation to his own religion and built a sacred city in the desert where he lived with his wife Nefertiti until their dreams ended in tragedy

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross demonstrates how to create the illusion of a mountain on a bright day mirrored in still waters

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Riviera's artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote d'Azur's beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent explores the sights, sounds and schedules of the Great British Summer, an epic journey that covers everything that informs viewers attitudes to summertime. She looks back at a range of influential television, from the travel shows of the sixties and seventies that first brought the world's finest resorts into our living rooms, to Del and Rodney Trotter fooling about abroad

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Former jockey Fred Quilley becomes convinced he's being hunted by a gang of crooks who once bribed him to fix a race. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, Felix Bowness and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A celebration of the British seaside holiday experience, from its Victorian origins and heyday in the 1950s, through its slow decline and attempts at modern-day reinvention

programme.title: Andrew Marr's Great Scots: The Writers Who Shaped a Nation
programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist looks into the life of 20th-century poet Hugh MacDiarmid, a staunch nationalist who reinvented Scots as a language for serious writing and tried to create an independent Scottish communist utopia. He was for many years under surveillance by MI5, was involved in plots to capture Edinburgh Castle and steal the Stone of Destiny, but also wrote the most powerful poetry in Scots since the days of Robert Burns, and started a Scottish renaissance that goes on to this day

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of 'Crazy' Fakhir Berwari, a Kurdish colonel in the Iraq Army and renowned bomb disposal expert, who single-handedly disarmed thousands of landmines across the country with just a pocketknife and a pair of wire clippers. Between the fall of Saddam Hussein in 2003 and the chaos and destruction wreaked by Isis 10 years later, Fakhir's unwavering bravery saved thousands of lives throughout his nation. This film tells Fakhir's story through the raw and visceral amateur footage captured by his soldiers on camcorder

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Emily takes Rosie on a disastrous trip to the swimming pool, leaving her trying to work out what Rosie really wants, while Simon's behaviour causes raised eyebrows from some of the other parents when he goes to take pictures of all the local playgrounds and paddling pools. In the earlier timeline, Rosie goes to her first genetics appointment where, in contrast to Emily's scientific detachment, Simon becomes angry that she is treated like an interesting puzzle to be solved

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Grandad John and Nana Annie want to take Ben away over the summer, but Simon and Emily want to know why they will not take Rosie as well. In the earlier timeline, Simon and Emily take the kids to Grandad John's for Christmas, but their relationship is at breaking point. As Emily sinks to her lowest ebb, it's Grandad John who finally gives her the support she needs
