programme.desc: Justin needs to fix Robert the Robot's memory chips when they accidentally come loose

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Raa Raa and his friends hear a strange noise and find a set of tracks in the Jungle, but what could be making them?

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb hopes to become a magician, but fails to impress his friends when he puts on a show for them

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: But while he is showing them what he can do, he accidentally rubs them out! Pug helps the pencil get them back to their original design.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny is heartbroken when his favourite book falls into the bath and gets wet, but Flop helps him understand that the story can still be told

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos move props on the theatre stage. Back on the Big Red Boat, the Octopus pays a visit and Peekaboo is sure there is something behind him

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends enjoy a steam train adventure, and Mr Tumble zooms off into space

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Laa-Laa and Po laugh themselves silly with a funny joke but when they tell it to Dipsy it doesn't tickle Dipsy in the same way. But Dipsy's funny dance does and when Dipsy dances again the Teletubbies all laugh together. In Tummy Tales, children have fun being silly in a park

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels are tidying up- its ‘Bring your Grandparents to Squirrel Club Day'. Duggee has his Grandparents badge so he knows what they will like - a nice cup of tea. But on arrival, these grandparents seem more of a lively bunch than Duggee was expecting

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters delve into Miss Grizzlesniff's dressing up chest. Wufflebump dresses up as a super monster, Icklewoo as a pirate, Wingston as an alien and Yummble as a cupcake. However, poor Meepa can't find a costume to fit her four monster arms. Can her friends use their imagination and a little bit of spider magic to help?

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid steps through the magic curtain to be a rock musician and play in a concert, but must find the missing singer before the show starts

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special autumn walk that starts in the daytime and finishes in the dark, where they all get to drink hot chocolate. On the way, they discover where a beaver has been at work, play inside a tree, make shadows with their lanterns and even fly like bats!

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Dog gets fleas, so the Patchwork Pals try to find a way to help him

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale, telling the story of why fleas jump

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Molly brings the flavours of the Caribbean to her home and cooks a Saint Lucian-themed meal for her friends

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep play at being pirates, but get a big surprise when they go on a treasure hunt

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah is inspired by listening to a poetry record and decides to make up some poetry of her own

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee would love a garden of his own that looks as amazing as Mr Og's. Mo does her best to help him create one, but gardens take time to grow

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It is O's Birthday but R has forgotten to get him a present, so tries to magic up a big wheel, helter skelter and hall of mirrors from thin air

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say book and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One finds a friendly furball, two of them tickle Two and soon Numberland is full of fun and fluffies! Have fun counting and learning how to make sevens with the Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The rabbit buys a present to give to Sula - but wants to keep it for himself instead

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The friends eat too much and feel full, and after seeing someone pour water into a glass, they get carried away finding their own containers to fill

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin spends the day with a radio star at her school. They record a rap for CBeebies Radio. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble and Grandad Tumble run Tumble FM Radio Show

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po drive the Tubby Car to the Hidey Hup where they discover the Tubby Uppy Downy. They all have fun going up and down

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: It's Snoof Air Show day and the excited Olobobs try flying a kite. When it gets stuck in a tree, they make Loopy Longtail reach up to the kite - though he then takes off with it

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo cannot find his Big Book of Animals and is keen to make sure that it is still exactly the same. This is where the Book Animals come from, so they want to find it too

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo finds choosing some things difficult, and that sometimes has no choice at all

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: In the first episode we meet four-year-old Ottalie who lives on a farm. Today Ottalie helps out, collecting fresh eggs from the chickens, cleaning out their run, and giving them new straw. Back inside it's time for a game of hide-and-seek with Mum and little sister Imogen. But when Dad gets home, will he give away Mum's hiding place?

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing building machines

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line help a little sea monster collect donations of gold for a 'Save the Sea Monsters' charity

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels decide it is time to tidy up. Luckily Duggee has his Organising Badge, and with the help of the Squirrels they reorganize everything in the clubhouse

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Felix and Nutkin quarrel, the Squirrel Camp is soon in chaos, and it falls to Peter to help them make up before disaster strikes. Adventures with the animal and his friends

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how digger tracks help it move and she finds out how wellies are made

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: On a camping trip to High Snowy Mountain, Bitz, Bob and Purl offer to help a very tired bear-sitter Bevel get two bear cubs to take their nap

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch gets into a mountain of trouble, as the Go Jetters attempt to climb the Matterhorn

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Poor Laila's been ill, she's not been out for days and now it's raining

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo has a sleepover at Gran Gran's, but bedtime comes while it's still light outside.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The art gallery holds a celebration of the colour pink and Sarah is keen to take part

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Each of the friends decide to give Lily Plant some pretend water'

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: As the air grows cold, a crystal-shaped seed lands on the Clangers' planet. Granny helps Tiny to plant it and it quickly grows into a crystal tree. Other seeds fall and soon there is a small forest. Narrated by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy dances in the garden and the Pinky Ponk copies her every move

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Jessica Ennis-Hill reads Puffin Peter, by Petr Horacek

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.desc: A veteran racing driver is involved in a horrific crash on the finish line, two friends fish a hand grenade out of a river and a young boy alerts his mum to a car fire

programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A revelation spreads through the residents, upsetting many and injuring relationships. Australian drama following life in an inner-city neighbourhood

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a home in the north Cornwall countryside with a budget of £525,000, and also visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield, but a messy encounter with a gull almost ruins one deal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a three-bedroom semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri help two teams search for items at Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, while presenter Anita Manning looks at the history of the game of marbles

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Baby Patch is taken away, and erratic Ash wants to earn some money, but needs a car to do it

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in south Wales on a budget of £600,000 and tries his hand at catching salmon using the art of lave net fishing in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists to create pieces for people they have never met, with the trio trying to work out the buyer's style and tastes by looking around their home, beginning by travelling to Lancashire to a renovated worker's cottage

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel to the Welsh border, while silver cutlery, a croquet set, and a 110-year-old bandaged teddy bear go up for auction in Burnley

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present a mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The double-length 30th anniversary episode of the soap, in which Lucy Beale's murderer is unmasked. Nick Cotton's body is found and the truth comes to light for a few residents. Dot is determined to do the right thing and takes matters into her own hands, despite her nearest and dearest trying to find a way to help. Dean sets his revenge plan into motion over at the Vic, Kim goes into premature labour and secrets are revealed about the Mitchells. The shocking true identity of Lucy's murderer comes to light, 10 months after she was killed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is concerned that charismatic locum Drew Nicholson-Heath (guest star John Barrowman) is out to expose him, so he resorts to his old tricks of playing the hero - but he is almost caught out and gaslights a colleague into thinking she made a mistake. A desperate Sacha wants to give Essie a reason to keep on living, but when she insists on preparing for the inevitable, he does something completely out of character. Kian spends the day babysitting Emma, hoping to win back Jac's heart

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes tries to make friends with Dermot's mother-in-law Hillary ahead of the baby's birth, and suggests they go out to dinner together. However, the evening comes to an abrupt end for the two feuding matriarchs when expectant mum Maria goes into labour, and the Browns are forced to rush her to hospital. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of giving up sex for a month, visiting a psychologist to understand her own desires and investigating the pressures young people experience in their sex lives owing to online porn. She meets a celibate rapper who intends to wait until his wedding night and gets some tips on finding alternative ways to connect with her partner

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle does not let lockdown get in the way of completing judging duties on a new season of Drag Race and arranging a high school graduation ceremony for Miss Rona. Fashion designer Marc Jacobs offers tips on staying creative, while comedian Celeste Barber explains the importance of a cupboard full of booze

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A mother and daughter suddenly find their tumble drier has set alight, and it all goes wrong for a top British surfer as he rides a wave

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall guide two teams scouring south-west London for bargains to take to auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to Strawberry Hill House in Twickenham, one of the finest examples of Gothic revival architecture in the UK

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A blood donor session sees some awkward encounters, while Ash annoys a customer. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

Dot is determined to do the right thing and takes matters into her own hands, despite her nearest and dearest trying to find a way to help. Dean sets his revenge plan into motion over at the Vic, Kim goes into premature labour and secrets are revealed about the Mitchells. The shocking true identity of Lucy's murderer comes to light, 10 months after she was killed

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is concerned that charismatic locum Drew Nicholson-Heath (guest star John Barrowman) is out to expose him, so he resorts to his old tricks of playing the hero - but he is almost caught out and gaslights a colleague into thinking she made a mistake. A desperate Sacha wants to give Essie a reason to keep on living, but when she insists on preparing for the inevitable, he does something completely out of character. Kian spends the day babysitting Emma, hoping to win back Jac's heart

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes tries to make friends with Dermot's mother-in-law Hillary ahead of the baby's birth, and suggests they go out to dinner together. However, the evening comes to an abrupt end for the two feuding matriarchs when expectant mum Maria goes into labour, and the Browns are forced to rush her to hospital. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Mark Daly investigates the impact of the coronavirus pandemic on care homes, examining what was done to look after the residents and whether care staff were adequately protected

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Michelle does not let lockdown get in the way of completing judging duties on a new season of Drag Race and arranging a high school graduation ceremony for Miss Rona. Fashion designer Marc Jacobs offers tips on staying creative, while comedian Celeste Barber explains the importance of a cupboard full of booze

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of giving up sex for a month, visiting a psychologist to understand her own desires and investigating the pressures young people experience in their sex lives owing to online porn. She meets a celibate rapper who intends to wait until his wedding night and gets some tips on finding alternative ways to connect with her partner

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.desc: A mother and daughter suddenly find their tumble drier has set alight, and it all goes wrong for a top British surfer as he rides a wave

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. Also, cameras follow Border Force officers intercepting potentially lethal medicines and members of the National Crime Agency as they raid the homes of currency counterfeiters. Presented by Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the sales of a terrace house in Hornchurch, a piece of land in Newquay, and a commercial space in Rochdale

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A veteran racing driver is involved in a horrific crash on the finish line, two friends fish a hand grenade out of a river and a young boy alerts his mum to a car fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A revelation spreads through the residents, upsetting many and injuring relationships. Australian drama following life in an inner-city neighbourhood

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a home in the north Cornwall countryside with a budget of £525,000, and also visits the annual Royal Cornwall Show

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion set out from Morecambe to hunt for items to sell at an auction in Sheffield, but a messy encounter with a gull almost ruins one deal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The presenter helps design a garden for the Montgomerys in Banbridge, Co Down, who have a spectacular view but a lacklustre garden to see it from. He is joined by renowned head gardener Darragh Stone to offer advice on what can be done with small spaces, and gives his mum's Dublin balcony a new lease of life

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Gillian Taylforth and Adam Woodyatt, aka mother and son Kathy and Ian Beale, who tell all about their unique working relationship and 35-year friendship. They talk about their biggest scenes - from Gillian's nerves while filming Kathy's shock return from the dead to the real reason Adam had to wear a hat throughout Ian's homeless storyline

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay return to showcase more films from the magazine programme that were made in Northern Ireland, beginning with a dolphin show at the Grove Leisure Centre, Blobby Bobbies and the mysterious healing soil of Fermanagh

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: A wildly successful music producer who is one of Speedi-Kazz's most loyal customers offers Kazim and Jeremiah the chance to use his studio for free - and they jump at the chance to record a promotional song for their delivery service

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Evan Biddell hosts as the contestants take on an eco-inspired design challenge, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. In the final episode of the series, Jamie Redknapp and Mo Farah go head to head in a spectacular Sporting Fix finale, and there is an extra special footballing legends edition of Musical VAR. Plus, AJ Tracey and Mabel give an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a three-bedroom semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Featuring a family trapped 50 metres up on a south coast tourist attraction, and a rock climber who disobeys his dad, with terrifying consequences. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: Of Diabolical Purpose - Sgeulachd Monsieur Chantrelle
programme.desc: Using dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion, John Morrison revisits the story of Eugene Chantrelle, a French teacher hanged for murdering his wife in Edinburgh in 1878. A gifted linguist with medical knowledge, he frequently threatened to poison his wife - and was accused of so doing upon her death. Chantrelle pleaded his innocence up to the moment he was hanged and the evidence against him was circumstancial - and his great-grandson speaks of the lasting damage caused to the family

programme.desc: Camus residents are stunned by shocking news, while others are forced to face the personal effects of the tragedy

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Blackpool, where Debbie Serpell tries to buy some Moorcroft pottery, James Layte has his eye on a power hammer and a selection of football medals look like being a bargain find. But which will the dealers clinch and which will go under the hammer?

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five Manchester market workers answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz hosted, by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Wendy, Alfred and Frances pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Drama unfolds in the garage, Cain and Moira grow closer, and Charity is frustrated by another dead end

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen wakes up after a heart attack, and Brian is annoyed that Bernie found a watch in the woods and sold it to Sarah. Meanwhile, Abi tells Ed that Aggie can use Kevin's car until hers is fixed as the neighbourhood is grateful for putting herself on the front line at the hospital

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's book group discusses Crime and Punishment at the house, which turns out to be surprisingly appropriate after Tracey warns Garth not to lead his younger brother astray. This episode features the return of Melanie Fishman, Dorien's rival from the original BBC series. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Jan Goodman star

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: After weeks of speculation, the Broadchurch community struggles to come to terms with the truth about the events surrounding Danny Latimer's death, and the revelations threaten to prevent the dead boy from being allowed to rest in peace. Crime drama, starring David Tennant, Olivia Colman, Andrew Buchan, Jodie Whittaker, Arthur Darvill, Vicky McClure and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Goals
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef and food writer Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to authentic Indian food, sharing her family's own recipes and visiting cooks across the UK and various regions of India. In the first episode, Parveen shares a family recipe for lamb bhuna with raita and rice and shares thoughts on keeping spices fresh. She also takes a trip to Delhi, sampling some traditional breakfast dishes and meeting the acclaimed chef behind some groundbreaking street food

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. An American tourist who visited Portwenn previously returns to the town, hoping to research her family tree. However, she also has a medical issue, and comes to the troubled doctor seeking his advice. Louisa goes to see Chris to find out how serious the situation is, while the townsfolk take steps to show Martin how much he is appreciated. Sigourney Weaver reprises her guest role from the previous series

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Unseen footage from the actress' travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Madeleine McCann: The Hunt for the Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Three-year-old Madeleine McCann disappeared from her parents' holiday apartment in Portugal 13 years ago, a mystery that has gripped the world. Now German prosecutors believe they have a prime suspect, a convicted sex offender who was living near the apartments at the time. But why has it taken a decade for him to be linked to the crime? Julie Etchingham investigates a trail that has involved police forces from at least three European countries

programme.title: Paul O'Grady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home. This time, there is a heart-warming update with Chester, the most injured dog Paul's ever helped, and Louis the Labrador, who now works at a medical detection school. Later, Paul discovers how Sydney, Battersea's most famous rescue and firm favourite of actor Tom Hardy, has coped in the outside world

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories

She also talks about cooking cow-udder pie for her first TV appearance more than 40 years ago, and reveals how she used to discipline her children

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Wendy, Alfred and Frances pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Drama unfolds in the garage, Cain and Moira grow closer, and Charity is frustrated by another dead end

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen wakes up after a heart attack, and Brian is annoyed that Bernie found a watch in the woods and sold it to Sarah. Meanwhile, Abi tells Ed that Aggie can use Kevin's car until hers is fixed as the neighbourhood is grateful for putting herself on the front line at the hospital

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien's book group discusses Crime and Punishment at the house, which turns out to be surprisingly appropriate after Tracey warns Garth not to lead his younger brother astray. This episode features the return of Melanie Fishman, Dorien's rival from the original BBC series. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Jan Goodman star

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: After weeks of speculation, the Broadchurch community struggles to come to terms with the truth about the events surrounding Danny Latimer's death, and the revelations threaten to prevent the dead boy from being allowed to rest in peace. Crime drama, starring David Tennant, Olivia Colman, Andrew Buchan, Jodie Whittaker, Arthur Darvill, Vicky McClure and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Goals
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a rundown of the best 50 goals from the FIFA World Cup, featuring memorable strikes from Michael Owen, Dennis Bergkamp, Diego Maradona and Pele among the contenders for the most unforgettable World Cup goal in the history of the tournament

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef and food writer Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to authentic Indian food, sharing her family's own recipes and visiting cooks across the UK and various regions of India. In the first episode, Parveen shares a family recipe for lamb bhuna with raita and rice and shares thoughts on keeping spices fresh. She also takes a trip to Delhi, sampling some traditional breakfast dishes and meeting the acclaimed chef behind some groundbreaking street food

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. An American tourist who visited Portwenn previously returns to the town, hoping to research her family tree. However, she also has a medical issue, and comes to the troubled doctor seeking his advice. Louisa goes to see Chris to find out how serious the situation is, while the townsfolk take steps to show Martin how much he is appreciated. Sigourney Weaver reprises her guest role from the previous series

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Unseen footage from the actress' travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: Madeleine McCann: The Hunt for the Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Three-year-old Madeleine McCann disappeared from her parents' holiday apartment in Portugal 13 years ago, a mystery that has gripped the world. Now German prosecutors believe they have a prime suspect, a convicted sex offender who was living near the apartments at the time. But why has it taken a decade for him to be linked to the crime? Julie Etchingham investigates a trail that has involved police forces from at least three European countries

programme.title: Paul O'Grady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home. This time, there is a heart-warming update with Chester, the most injured dog Paul's ever helped, and Louis the Labrador, who now works at a medical detection school. Later, Paul discovers how Sydney, Battersea's most famous rescue and firm favourite of actor Tom Hardy, has coped in the outside world

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

She also talks about cooking cow-udder pie for her first TV appearance more than 40 years ago, and reveals how she used to discipline her children

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve Crane's wayward mother Babs arrives in Aidensfield, having fallen out with her screenwriter boyfriend and grown tired of the London arts scene. She causes all manner of chaos when a spate of burglaries provides her with the ideal opportunity to put her self-defence skills into practice. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps has an amazing change in fortune. Rural drama, starring James Carlton

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Ruth talks her cantankerous landlord Rigsby into renting his newly vacated room to a friend of hers. Pilot episode of the classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A dangerous ex-con returns to Aidensfield hoping to exact revenge on Merton, the officer who apprehended him, and takes Jenny hostage. Meanwhile, the village is rocked by a spate of car thefts and Vernon organises a charity auction. Drama, starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Paul Brennan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv is furious about catching Terry with a mystery blonde. Ned worries about Jan who refuses to accept that Dave is dead. Tom and Kelly get intimate in a barn

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is upset to see Kathy with Sean. Albert tells Lisa about the bet, and she vows to get revenge on Zak. Viv is worried that Terry no longer wants to commit to her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is pleased to see Alec, but Steve is keen for him to go. Mike signs the garage over to Don. Kevin tells Don that Mike had offered the garage to him for only £40,000

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley and Kelly arrange to have a date in the Rovers. Tricia is not happy when Josie labels Jamie a thief. Maureen is sick of Maud getting at her

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A dangerous ex-con returns to Aidensfield hoping to exact revenge on Merton, the officer who apprehended him, and takes Jenny hostage. Meanwhile, the village is rocked by a spate of car thefts and Vernon organises a charity auction. Drama, starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Paul Brennan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Lonely landlord Rigsby decides he needs help to improve his ailing love life and turns to new tenant Philip for advice. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby wines and dines Miss Jones in a suit surreptitiously 'borrowed' from stroppy wrestler Spooner - who turns up halfway through the main course intent on exacting revenge. Vintage comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Meanwhile, a reluctant Bernie is taken on a mini break to a country hotel by Vernon - but he grows increasingly dismayed over the plans for a new garage. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Dave's funeral arrives. Zak attempts to win back Lisa's favour. Kathy attacks Kim when she turns up at the funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Kim she is not wanted at the funeral. Marlon and Butch vie for Sophie's attention. Roy tries to smash up Kim's car and blames her for killing his brother

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Joyce tries to persuade Vera to take on Judy as a barmaid. Don lays down the law when Kevin questions his authority. Liz, Deirdre, Audrey, Alma and Maureen have a girls' night in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is furious when Steve patronises her. The men go to see Betty's Hot Shot race at Thirsk. Maureen is stunned to see a for sale sign up outside Reg's flat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Meanwhile, a reluctant Bernie is taken on a mini break to a country hotel by Vernon - but he grows increasingly dismayed over the plans for a new garage. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby asks Philip for a native charm to help him seduce the delectable Ruth - but his dreams of a romantic liaison soon go up in smoke. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens tell Rachel about their financial problems. Vic invites Viv to dinner to discuss their children. Steve finally tells Rachel how he really feels about her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic appears very tolerant of the idea of Donna moving in with Viv and Terry. Chris offers to buy Kim out of the stables and the golf course. Jack is paid a visit by Andy's teacher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve thinks the police are using Vicky to get to him. Jim appears in court. Vicky turns to Alec for help, telling him everything. Maureen claims Reg's flat as her marital home.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve visits Malcolm Fox to find out what is going on. Maureen has the locks changed on Reg's flat. Ashley and Kelly plan to spend the night together when Ken goes away on a course

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor is found dead on Halloween with a stake through her heart and Hobson reveals she shared a house with the victim in her university days. A medium visits Lewis claiming to have foreseen the tragedy, and ghostly messages spelled out in fridge magnet letters start appearing in a house occupied by four students. As the detective struggles with the possibility his friend is somehow implicated in the crime, a tale of jealousy and revenge begins to unfold. Rupert Graves (Sherlock) and Niamh Cusack (Heartbeat) guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.desc: Harriet is completely floored by Malone's admission, he and Will come to blows, and Charity makes a deal

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek uses a camcorder to record the transformation of the allotment. Jack's day at the races leads to the men winning big, and he knows what they should invest their money in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is furious after hearing Martin has invested in a racehorse, Maureen is sick of everyone being supportive of her, and Vicky is stunned when Alec arrives to stay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: An ambulance races Dave to hospital, Frank stops Kim from seeing the patient, and Biff and Linda's wedding reception is interrupted as news about Dave filters through

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank offers to pay for Dave's funeral, but Ned refuses, and Biff and Linda confront Kim about what happened to Dave. Zak declares 1996 as one of the worst years of his life

Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Meanwhile, a reluctant Bernie is taken on a mini break to a country hotel by Vernon - but he grows increasingly dismayed over the plans for a new garage. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Joyce tries to persuade Vera to take on Judy as a barmaid. Don lays down the law when Kevin questions his authority. Liz, Deirdre, Audrey, Alma and Maureen have a girls' night in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is furious when Steve patronises her. The men go to see Betty's Hot Shot race at Thirsk. Maureen is stunned to see a for sale sign up outside Reg's flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Dave's funeral arrives. Zak attempts to win back Lisa's favour. Kathy attacks Kim when she turns up at the funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Kim she is not wanted at the funeral. Marlon and Butch vie for Sophie's attention. Roy tries to smash up Kim's car and blames her for killing his brother

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children.
